title: DM effects shall be independent of federate logical time.
Each FDD class shall have at most one immediate superclass.
Object classes shall expose declared and inherited available attributes.
Interaction classes shall expose declared and inherited available parameters.
RTI shall track published object-class attributes per joined federate.
RTI shall track subscribed object-class attributes per joined federate.
RTI shall track published interaction classes per joined federate.
RTI shall track subscribed interaction classes per joined federate.
RTI shall support interaction between DM subscriptions and DDM subscriptions.
RTI shall support subscribing with update rate reduction where applicable.
notes: The backend now proves explicit and FOM-declared update-rate defaults across direct, inherited, and regioned subscriptions, plus receive-order non-suppression when no logical-time basis exists. Broader vendor-style update-rate policies remain outside the current model.

title: RTI shall provide Publish Object Class Attributes.
Publish Object Class Attributes shall declare attributes a federate may update for instances of an object class.
RTI shall reject publication requests for unavailable attributes.
RTI shall provide Unpublish Object Class Attributes.
Unpublishing shall remove the federate’s ability to update the specified attributes.
RTI shall provide Publish Interaction Class.
Publication of an interaction class shall permit the federate to send interactions of that class.
RTI shall provide Unpublish Interaction Class.
Unpublishing an interaction class shall remove the federate’s ability to send interactions of that class.
RTI shall provide Subscribe Object Class Attributes.
Subscribing to object-class attributes shall make matching object instances discoverable when discovery conditions are met.
Subscribing to object-class attributes shall make matching in-scope attribute updates reflectable.
RTI shall provide Unsubscribe Object Class Attributes.
Unsubscribing shall remove the federate’s interest in the specified object-class attributes.
RTI shall provide Subscribe Interaction Class.
Subscribing to an interaction class shall make matching interactions receivable.
RTI shall provide Unsubscribe Interaction Class.
RTI shall invoke Start Registration For Object Class when registration becomes useful for subscribed federates.
RTI shall invoke Stop Registration For Object Class when registration is no longer useful for subscribed federates.
RTI shall invoke Turn Interactions On when a published interaction class has matching subscribers.
RTI shall invoke Turn Interactions Off when a published interaction class no longer has matching subscribers.
RTI shall support registration, modification, and deletion of object instances.
RTI shall support sending and receiving interactions.
RTI shall discover object instances at subscribed federates when discovery conditions are satisfied.
RTI shall determine a candidate discovery class from the registered class or closest subscribed superclass.
Once discovered, an object instance’s discovered class shall not change.
A registered or discovered object instance shall become known to the joined federate.
RTI shall determine whether an instance attribute is in scope for each joined federate.
RTI shall reflect attribute updates only when attributes are in scope and subscribed.
RTI shall not reflect out-of-scope attribute updates except where required by service semantics.
RTI shall determine attribute relevance from publication, subscription, ownership, and scope.

title: RTI shall handle orphan object instances according to ownership and discovery rules.
RTI shall deliver interactions to joined federates subscribed to the relevant interaction class.
RTI shall use transportation types for object updates and interactions as defined by the FDD and service arguments.
notes: This broad standards row remains partial. The backend now proves distinct reliable versus best-effort callback transport behavior for both FOM-defined defaults and explicit service-selected overrides, plus restore persistence for the supported subset, but it still does not model the full custom transportation semantic space described by the standard.

title: RTI shall support explicit reliable transportation type selection, query reporting, and restore persistence for object updates and interactions in the currently supported transport subset.
notes: This narrow supported-subset row covers explicit reliable transport selection, report/query callbacks, and restore persistence for object and interaction transport state.

title: RTI shall support distinct best-effort versus reliable delivery semantics for object updates and interactions when explicit transportation selections differ.
notes: This supported-subset row is now implemented: explicit reliable versus best-effort transport overrides produce distinct callback transport handles for object updates and interactions, and that non-reliable override state survives restore.

title: RTI may combine, package, or passelize messages without changing externally visible semantics.
notes: This backend proves externally visible delivery semantics for unbatched callbacks. It does not explicitly model message combination, packaging, or passelization, so this row remains partial by policy until those semantics are modeled or separately justified.

title: RTI shall honor update-rate reduction when reflecting attribute updates.
notes: The backend proves explicit and FOM-declared update-rate throttling for direct, inherited, and regioned subscriptions, plus receive-order non-suppression when no logical-time basis exists. Broader vendor-style update-rate policies remain outside the current model, so this row remains partial.
